title: Nature Reuses and Recycles
titleZh: 大自然再利用与再循环

q.en: What is the difference between recycling and reusing, according to the book?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，回收（recycling）和再利用（reusing）有什么区别？

options.en: Recycling breaks materials down to make something new, while reusing keeps the item in the same form to use again
options.zh: 回收是把材料分解后制成新东西，再利用是保持物品原样再次使用

options.en: Recycling is only for plants according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 回收只针对植物（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

explain.en: The book says recycling means materials 'can be remade into a new thing,' while reusing means 'you use it again. You keep it in the same form.'
explain.zh: 书中说，回收意味着材料“可以被重新制成新东西”，而再利用是“再次使用它，保持原有形态”。

q.en: According to the book, how many different uses does it list for a tree's leaves?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，它列出了树叶的几种不同用途？

options.en: Two according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 两种（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

options.en: Five (food for the tree, shade for a plant, home for a bird, home for a squirrel, home for an insect)
options.zh: 五种（为树提供食物、为植物遮荫、为鸟安家、为松鼠安家、为昆虫安家）

explain.en: The book lists five uses: leaves give 'food for the tree, shade for a plant, a home for a bird, a home for a squirrel, and a home for an insect. That's five uses for the same leaves!'
explain.zh: 书中列出五种用途：叶子为“树提供食物、为植物提供遮荫、为鸟提供家、为松鼠提供家、为昆虫提供家。同一片叶子有五种用途！”

options.en: Fallen trees that provide food and a protected place for new trees and plants to grow
options.zh: 倒下的树，为新树和其他植物提供食物和受保护的生长空间

options.en: Logs used to build houses according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 用来盖房子的原木（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says fallen trees 'become "nurse logs" that provide food and a protected place to grow for new trees and other plants.'
explain.zh: 书中说倒下的树“变成‘保育木’，为新树和其他植物提供食物和受保护的生长空间。”

q.en: What tiny organisms feed on dead things like fallen trees and break them down?
q.zh: 什么微小的生物以倒下的树等死物为食并把它们分解？

options.en: Squirrels according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 松鼠（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Bacteria (tiny organisms too small to see) and mushrooms
options.zh: 细菌（肉眼难见的微小生物）和蘑菇

explain.en: The book says 'Mushrooms and organisms too small to see, which are called bacteria, feed on dead things such as fallen trees' and break down their organic materials.
explain.zh: 书中说“蘑菇和肉眼难见、叫作细菌的生物，以倒下的树等死物为食”，并分解它们的有机物质。

q.en: What do earthworms leave behind that helps plants?
q.zh: 蚯蚓留下什么来帮助植物？

options.en: Waste (little blobs of soil) rich in nutrients
Leaves according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 叶子（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Earthworms' waste 'look like little blobs of soil, and in fact, these blobs are rich food for plants' full of nutrients plants need to grow.
explain.zh: 蚯蚓的排泄物“看起来像小土块，实际上却是植物的丰富养料”，富含植物生长所需的养分。

options.en: Made of plastic according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 由塑料制成（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines organic as 'related to living things.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将organic定义为“与生物有关的”。

q.en: Put the nutrient cycle in order: which comes right after a plant dies?
q.zh: 把养分循环按顺序排：植物死亡之后紧接着发生什么？

options.en: Earthworms eat the dead plant parts, and their waste makes nutrients for new plants
options.zh: 蚯蚓吃掉枯死的植物部分，它们的排泄物为新植物提供养分

options.en: Animals eat the living plant according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 动物吃掉活着的植物（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: The plant turns into a rock

explain.en: The nutrient cycle: after 'The plants die,' 'Earthworms eat the dead plant parts' and 'Earthworms make waste that mixes with soil' to feed new plants.
explain.zh: 养分循环：在“植物死亡”之后，“蚯蚓吃掉枯死的植物部分”，并“制造混入土壤的排泄物”来滋养新植物。

q.en: What do beavers use trees for?

options.en: They cut down trees and branches to make their homes and dams
options.zh: 它们砍倒树木和树枝，来建造自己的家和堤坝

options.en: They use them as umbrellas according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它们把树当伞用（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says 'Beavers cut down trees and branches to make their homes and dams.'
explain.zh: 书中说“河狸砍倒树木和树枝，来建造自己的家和堤坝。”

q.en: The book explains the word part 're-' in recycle means:
q.zh: 书中解释说recycle里的字根“re-”意思是：

options.en: 'Before' according to some versions of the story

explain.en: The Word Wise box says, 'The word part re- means "again." The word part -cycle means to circle around.'
explain.zh: 书中“词语智慧”框说：“re-这个字根意思是‘再次’；-cycle字根意思是绕圈。”

q.en: What lesson does the author want readers to learn from nature?
q.zh: 作者希望读者从大自然中学到什么道理？

options.en: That dead trees are useless according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 枯死的树毫无用处（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

options.en: Nature reuses and recycles every part of every living thing, and people can learn from it to create less trash
options.zh: 大自然再利用、再循环每一个生物的每一部分，人可以向它学习，少制造垃圾

explain.en: The book concludes, 'nature reuses or recycles every part of every living thing. We can learn a lot from nature,' and people reuse/recycle to 'create less trash.'
explain.zh: 书中总结：“大自然再利用或再循环每一个生物的每一部分。我们能从大自然学到很多”，而人们再利用、再循环是为了“少制造垃圾”。

prompt.en: Look around your home or yard and name one natural material that gets reused or recycled in nature (like a fallen leaf or dead log). Explain how it helps other living things.
prompt.zh: 观察你的家或院子，说出一种在自然界中被再利用或再循环的自然物质（比如落叶或枯木）。解释它如何帮助其他生物。

en: Name a specific natural material (leaf, log, dead plant, etc.).
Describe at least one living thing that benefits from it, using an idea from the book.
zh: 说出一种具体的自然物质（叶子、原木、枯死的植物等）。
用书中一个观点，描述至少一种从中受益的生物。
